title: Vepris lanceolata
taxonomy: Vepris lanceolata is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae. This family is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The Rutaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes a wide range of species, many of which are used for their fragrant oils and flavors. Vepris lanceolata is native to tropical Africa and is often used in traditional medicine. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Vepris and the subfamily Aurantioideae, which is a subfamily within the Rutaceae family.
uses: Vepris lanceolata is known to have various medicinal uses due to its rich content of bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used in African cultures to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. Its leaves and bark are often used in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms of malaria and other diseases. Extracts from Vepris lanceolata have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and its potential applications in modern medicine.

intro: Vepris lanceolata, also known as the African ironwood, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vepris lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In traditional medicine, Vepris lanceolata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used in the form of tea, tincture, or powder to treat various health conditions.
